content.Text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 12:43 PDT] Create a new file `docs/protocol-compare.html` — a self-contained, single-file HTML page (all CSS and JS inline) for BioBots: "Protocol Comparison Engine".

## Design Pattern
Follow the exact same dark-theme design system as other pages in docs/:
- CSS variables: --bg: #0f172a; --surface: #1e293b; --border: #334155; --text: #e2e8f0; --muted: #94a3b8; --accent: #3b82f6; --green: #22c55e; --red: #ef4444; --yellow: #eab308;
- Font: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif
- Card-based layout with .card class
- Header with title, accent-colored span, subtitle
- Responsive grid layout

## Feature: Protocol Comparison Engine
A tool for comparing two bioprinting protocols side-by-side with intelligent analysis.

### Core Features:
1. **Two Protocol Input Panels** — Each panel lets user define a protocol with these parameters:
   - Material (dropdown: GelMA, Alginate, Collagen, PEGDA, Fibrin, Silk Fibroin, Matrigel, Chitosan)
   - Nozzle Diameter (µm): range 100-800
   - Print Speed (mm/s): range 1-50
   - Temperature (°C): range 15-45
   - Layer Height (µm): range 50-500
   - Infill Density (%): range 10-100
   - Crosslink Time (s): range 5-300
   - Cell Density (M cells/mL): range 0.1-50
   - Pressure (kPa): range 5-200
   - UV Exposure (s): range 0-120 (0 = none)

2. **3 Preset Protocols** per panel (dropdown to quick-load):
   - "Standard GelMA Scaffold" — GelMA, 400µm, 10mm/s, 25°C, 200µm, 60%, 30s, 5M, 50kPa, 15s UV
   - "High-Resolution Collagen" — Collagen, 150µm, 3mm/s, 37°C, 100µm, 80%, 0s, 10M, 30kPa, 0s UV
   - "Fast Alginate Prototype" — Alginate, 600µm, 25mm/s, 22°C, 350µm, 40%, 60s, 2M, 80kPa, 0s UV

3. **Side-by-Side Diff View** — After clicking "Compare", show a table with:
   - Each parameter row
   - Protocol A value | Protocol B value
   - Delta column (absolute and % difference)
   - Color-coded: green if same/similar (<10% diff), yellow if moderate (10-30%), red if large (>30%)
   - Material row: highlighted if different materials

4. **Smart Merge Recommendations** (the agentic part) — Below the diff table, show an "AI Recommendations" card with:
   - For each parameter with significant difference, recommend the optimal value with reasoning
   - Use heuristic rules like:
     - Lower print speed + smaller nozzle = better resolution but slower
     - Higher cell density needs lower pressure to avoid shear damage
     - GelMA needs UV crosslinking, Alginate needs CaCl2 (chemical)
     - Temperature should match material requirements
   - Overall compatibility score (0-100) for merging the two protocols
   - Risk flags: "⚠️ Shear stress risk if pressure >100kPa with cells >10M/mL"
   - A "Generate Merged Protocol" button that creates a recommended merged protocol

5. **Merged Protocol Output** — When generated, show a third column in the diff table with the merged values, plus:
   - Confidence level for each merged value (High/Medium/Low)
   - Export as JSON button

6. **Protocol History** — Store last 5 comparisons in localStorage, show as clickable list

### UI Layout:
- Header bar at top
- Two side-by-side cards for Protocol A and Protocol B input
- "Compare Protocols" button centered between them
- Diff results table (full width card below)
- Recommendations card below that
- History sidebar or collapsible section

### Quality:
- All interactive, no external dependencies
- Smooth transitions
- Mobile responsive (stack vertically on small screens)
- Include a back link "← Back to Hub" pointing to hub.html

Also update `docs/sitemap.xml`: add a new `<url>` entry for `protocol-compare.html` following the same pattern as existing entries.

Also update `docs/hub.html`: add "Protocol Comparison Engine" to the appropriate tools section. Look at how existing tools are listed and add it in a similar card/link format. Use 🔀 as the emoji icon and description "Side-by-side protocol diff with smart merge recommendations".

Run `node -c docs/shared/commandPalette.js` as a sanity check that node works, then verify your new HTML is valid by checking it has proper closing tags.
